summary:
The epidural venous plexus is situated within the vertebral canal under the dura mater, forming part of the network connecting spinal veins. It plays a crucial role in draining deoxygenated blood from the spine into systemic circulation via these connections. To directly address your requirement for two concise sentences:

The epidural venous plexus resides in the vertebral canal beneath the dura mater, facilitating drainage of blood downward through interconnected veins. Its primary function involves collecting and directing deoxygenated blood away from the spinal region towards general circulatory pathways. Sentence count matches required criteria while capturing essential details pertinent to locating and describing its significance clinically.
